-- 1 of 6 --

Short Term vs. Long Term
Reinforcement
S 	We meet needs in the short term, medium term, and long
term.
S 	Long-Term Reinforcment – your needs are met by actions
that are good for you in the long term.
S 	Often involves sacrifice or delayed gratification in the short
term as a trade off for greater gains later.
S 	Long-term reinforcement requires faith in the long plan.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 2 of 6 --

Short Term vs. Long Term
Reinforcement
S 	Short-Term Reinforcements – your needs are met here and
now.
S 	Often this takes place against a backdrop of desperation and
hunger. Often short-term reinforcements are destructive to
your long-term plans.
S 	This can become addictive, even if humiliating, and your life
situation can be warped by the need for short-term
reinforcement.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 6 --

When the Long Plan fails
S 	When someone has a shock, or tragedy, or injustice, they
can lose faith in the long plan for their life, and they give up
long-term rewards for short-term comforts.
S 	People may drop their standards for themselves and say
“screw it” and just go for short term, which usually leads to
even worse life circumstances.
S 	The coaching paradox: how to get someone to let go of
short-term reinforcements in favor of the long-term rewards,
even though it’s not rewarding now.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 6 --

Forces that will turn
John around
S 	The respect of his kids
S 	The self-respect of rising to a challenge
S 	Accepting and appreciating his blessings/advantages
S 	Succeeding as a father.
S 	When Tony learns that John quit drugs for his kids, he gave
John tremendous respect.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com
